title: Own developed widget in Docs - filter feature

posts.author: yarhuoy (Ng Yar Huoy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

The widgets in the screenshot are added in the Doc.

“Custom Calendar Widget” is our own custom developed widget, I can’t seem to find a way to enable the “Filter” feature in the widget (like the filter shown in Battery Widget), can you please advise on this?

g-company.monday.com

Welcome to monday.com | a new way of working

monday.com Work OS is an open platform where anyone can create the tools they need to run every aspect of their work.

image1571×837 53.6 KB

Thanks!

Br YarHuoy

posts.author: Dani (Dani Lester)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @yarhuoy

Thanks for your question!
As this concerns a Custom built app, I’m going to move your request to the Developer section of the Community.
This is managed by our Developer Success Team who will be able to continue to assist!

Best,

Dani

posts.author: yarhuoy (Ng Yar Huoy)

Thanks for your reponse! Is there any update from the team yet?

Br, YarHuoy

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @yarhuoy!

Matias from the Developer Success team here!

Looking forward to hearing from you!

posts.author: marcelmacha (Marcel Mauricio Moran Calderon)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Monday team,

I am having the same issue, I am not sure how to add the filter in my custom dashboard widget as well.

Many thanks in advance,

  • Marcel

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @marcelmacha and welcome to the community!

I hope you like it here

As of today, we do not support native filtering in widgets. Only in board views.

If you want to build some filtering logic, you will need to implement it on your end.

Hope this answers the question

Cheers,
Matias
